_all_text: Sunstone sunstone Variable: (Na,Ca)(Al,Si)AlSi2O8 (oligoclase/labradorite) or KAlSi3O8 (orthoclase) Oregon sunstone is a labradorite-composition plagioclase (An45–An68) with native copper inclusions. Norwegian sunstone is oligoclase with hematite platelets. Indian and other commercial sunstone is typically oligoclase with hematite or goethite inclusions. Copper-bearing Oregon sunstone is unique globally. Aventurescence in sunstone appears as a glittering, spangling metallic flash — brighter and more dramatic than the soft glow of adularescence in moonstone. Oregon sunstone is the only known feldspar with native copper inclusions, giving it a uniquely warm, saturated color. The metallic sparkle in Norwegian sunstone has a coppery-gold quality. The term 'sólarsteinn' (sunstone) from Norse sagas refers to a navigational crystal, which has generated scholarly debate. Most evidence points to calcite (Iceland spar) or cordierite as the actual navigational stone, not feldspar sunstone — the overlap of the trade name with the saga reference is a modern confusion. solar-energy vitality warmth abundance Norwegian sunstone historically collected and used as ornamental stone Oregon sunstone designated official state gemstone of Oregon in 1987 Viking sunstone (sólarsteinn): some researchers propose that Viking navigators used a transparent birefringent crystal — possibly calcite (Iceland spar) or cordierite — as a navigation tool; rarely associated with feldspar sunstone sunstone Oregon sunstone aventurescent feldspar copper feldspar schiller feldspar solar gem oligoclase sunstone feldspar aventurescent optical-effect variety gemstone copper solar oregon solar-fragment copper-harvest warm-metallic-scatter autumn-mineral orange golden-yellow salmon copper-red
